text: The product shall plot data points in a scientifically correct manner.
The product shall label grid axes correctly according to the input from the data file.
The product shall color data points in accordance with the cluster number contained in the data file.
The product shall support a maximum of 2000 data points.
The product shall display the name of a data point upon a single mouse click over the point.
The product shall display all details of a data point upon a double-click over the point.
The product shall allow the selection of multiple data points to display multiple names.
The product shall provide rotation functions to orient the grid.
The product shall provide zoom functions to orient the grid.
The product shall provide move functions to orient the grid.
The product shall support data files containing a name for each data point.
The product shall support data files containing three parameters for plotting each data point.
The product shall support data files containing a single parameter to designate the color of each point.
The product shall support data files containing a description for each data point.
The product shall allow the user to specify custom axis headings via the first row of the data file.
The product must be available on both Windows and Linux operating platforms.
The execution of the application must be maintained on the server side.
The product should run on a minimum of a mid-range personal computer.
The product shall operate with a mouse and keyboard as minimum input tools.
The product points should be sized to remain visible and selectable without distorting the overall pattern.
The product shall be colored to allow clear printing of black text and points on a white background.
The product should be intuitive and require no specialist training for use.
The product shall start within 30 seconds.
The product interaction with data points shall have a delay of no longer than 2 seconds.
The product response to orientation changes shall be fast enough to avoid interrupting the user's flow of thought.
